reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based on a frontend tracking link irrelevant to build/install security. PKGBUILD and install scripts show no malicious behavior during makepkg execution.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged a frontend tracking link as a supply-chain risk, while deepseek correctly focused on build/install security.
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed the FFmpeg bundle (critical binary executed at runtime)
Qwen missed PKGBUILD's prepare()/package() implementation details
false_positives: qwen's 'unsafe' verdict on libraryoptionseditor.js: Tracking link is an upstream privacy issue, not a build/install risk
false_negatives: Both missed potential runtime risks in un-reviewed binaries (FFmpeg)
Deepseek didn't verify service file's supplementaryGroups duplication impact
learnings: Prioritize binaries executed at runtime over frontend code
Improve model training on AUR-specific threat scope (build/install vs runtime)
Enforce review of all PKGBUILD functions and installed binaries
Add binary analysis capability for compiled dependencies
